_**Agent (default)**_

Here is how I will treat your project from here on.

**What your situation implies**

- **Development is live** — anything we merge or deploy can affect real users or stakeholders.
- **“Working things” must stay working** — prefer small, targeted edits, reuse existing patterns, and avoid drive-by refactors or broad rewrites unless you explicitly want them.

**How I will work when you ask for changes**

1. **Scope tightly** — change only what is needed for the request; leave unrelated code alone.
2. **Match the codebase** — same naming, structure, and patterns as surrounding code.
3. **Reason about risk** — touch auth, payments, routing, env/config, or shared components with extra care.
4. **Verify when possible** — run the project’s usual checks (lint, tests, build) after non-trivial edits if the repo supports them.
5. **Call out tradeoffs** — if something could affect production-like behavior, I will say so briefly before or after the change.

When you have a specific task (bug, feature, or refactor), describe it and I will implement it with this in mind. If you use feature flags, staging-only deploys, or a particular branch flow (e.g. `main` vs `development`), mention that once and I will align with it.

            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "/**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": " * POST /api/user/change-password"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": " * Change user password"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": " */"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "router.post("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "  '/change-password',"
          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

          },

            "text": "  async (req, res) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "    try {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "      const { currentPassword, newPassword } = req.body;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "      const userId = req.user.uid;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "      // Fetch user from Firestore"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "      const userDoc = await db.collection('users').doc(userId).get();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 207,
            "text": "      if (!userDoc.exists) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208,
            "text": "        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'User not found' });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 209,
            "text": "      }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 210,
            "text": "      const user = userDoc.data();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 211
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 212,
            "text": "      // Verify current password"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 213,
            "text": "      const passwordMatch = await bcrypt.compare(currentPassword, user.passwordHash);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 214,
            "text": "      if (!passwordMatch) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 215,
            "text": "        return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Current password is incorrect' });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 216,
            "text": "      }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 217
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 218,
            "text": "      // Hash new password"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 219,
            "text": "      const newPasswordHash = await bcrypt.hash(newPassword, 12);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 220
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 221,
            "text": "      // Update password in Firebase Auth"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 222,
            "text": "      await auth.updateUser(userId, {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 223,
            "text": "        password: newPassword"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 224,
            "text": "      });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 225
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 226,
            "text": "      // Update password hash in Firestore"
          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 375,
            "text": "// VERIFY MFA CODE endpoint - 🔴 UPDATED TO SET ACCOUNT TO ACTIVE"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 376,
            "text": "router.post('/mfa/verify', verifyJWT, async (req, res) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 377,
            "text": "  try {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 378,
            "text": "    const { uid } = req.user;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 379,
            "text": "    const { code } = req.body;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 380
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "    if (!code || !/^\\d{6}$/.test(code)) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382,
            "text": "      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid code format' });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 383,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 384
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 385,
            "text": "    const userDoc = await db.collection('users').doc(uid).get();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 386,
            "text": "    const user = userDoc.data();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 387
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "    // Verify TOTP code"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 389,
            "text": "    const verified = speakeasy.totp.verify({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 390,
            "text": "      secret: user.mfaSecret,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 391,
            "text": "      encoding: 'base32',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 392,
            "text": "      token: code,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 393,
            "text": "      window: 2"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 395
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 396,
            "text": "    if (!verified) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 397,
            "text": "      return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid MFA code' });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 398,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 399
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 400,
            "text": "    // 🔴 UPDATE: Mark MFA as verified, enabled, AND set account status to ACTIVE"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 401,
            "text": "    await db.collection('users').doc(uid).update({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 402,
            "text": "      mfaEnabled: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 403,
            "text": "      mfaVerified: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 404,
            "text": "      mfaRequired: false,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 405,
            "text": "      accountStatus: 'active',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 406,
            "text": "      mfaActivatedAt: new Date(),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 407,
            "text": "      updatedAt: new Date()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 408,
            "text": "    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 409
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 410,
            "text": "    // Log MFA setup completion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 411,
            "text": "    await auditLog({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 412,
            "text": "      userId: uid,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 413,
            "text": "      action: 'MFA_SETUP_COMPLETE',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 414,
            "text": "      details: { "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 415,
            "text": "        email: user.email,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 416,
            "text": "        accountStatus: 'active',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 417,
            "text": "        timestamp: new Date().toISOString() "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 418,
            "text": "      },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 419,
            "text": "      ipAddress: req.ip,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420,
            "text": "      userAgent: req.get('user-agent')"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421,
            "text": "    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 422
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "    res.json({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 424,
            "text": "      success: true,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 425,
            "text": "      message: 'MFA enabled successfully. Your account is now active!',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 426,
            "text": "      accountStatus: 'active',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 427,
            "text": "      mfaRequired: false"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428,
            "text": "    });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 429
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 430,
            "text": "  } catch (error) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 431,
            "text": "    console.error('MFA verification error:', error);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 432,
            "text": "    res.status(500).json({ error: 'MFA verification failed' });"
          },
          {

            "text": "/**"

          },

          },

          },

          },

          },

          },

            "text": "    // Generate full tokens"

          {
            "lineNumber": 471
          },

            "text": "    // Store session"
          },

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture. **requireMFA** checks `req.user.mfaVerified` on the JWT, but Firebase Auth's `generateTokens` likely doesn't include `mfaVerified` in the access token payload. When **requireMFA** fails, it returns a 403 error with `{ error: 'MFA verification required' }`, but no `tempToken` is provided. The settings page shows an MFA prompt for both 403 status and `data.mfaRequired`, yet `tempToken` is never returned from change-password or change-email endpoints. As a result, `handleFinalVerify` sends `token: null` to verify-mfa, which is incorrect since that's not the proper endpoint. The **verify-mfa route doesn't exist**  only `/api/auth/mfa/verify-login` exists, expecting `tempToken` + `code` from the login flow with temp JWT.

**Solution approach:**

- Option A  **Backend**: Implement proper step-up MFA for sensitive operations:
  - New endpoint or modified flow: `POST /api/user/change-password/initiate` returns 200 with challenge, or
  - Simpler: Remove `requireMFA` from sensitive routes and verify TOTP in the same request body `{ ..., mfaCode }` - Option A: Implement a new endpoint like `POST /api/user/change-password/initiate` to handle MFA verification during sensitive actions, or simplify by verifying TOTP in the same request body.
- Option B: Align with existing login MFA flow by returning a step-up temp token on 403 errors, requiring a new endpoint `POST /api/auth/mfa/verify-sensitive` to validate the code and issue an action token or updated JWT.

The cleanest minimal-disruption solution involves:
1. Returning a step-up temp token when `requireMFA` fails, enabling frontend to verify MFA via a new endpoint.
2. Creating `POST /api/auth/mfa/verify-sensitive` to accept the temp token and MFA code, returning an action token or refreshed JWT with `mfaVerified: true`.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see a bug in page.tsx - when login succeeds without MFA, it still sets currentStep to 'mfa-verify'. The bug occurs because `requireMFA` middleware always returns 403 for change-password and change-email routes. This happens since the JWT token contains `{ uid, type: 'access' }` without an `mfaVerified` field, making `req.user.mfaVerified` always undefined/falsy. 

The middleware execution order is correct, but the flow is:
- User submits change password
- verifyJWT passes (sets req.user with uid)
- requireMFA fails due to missing mfaVerified  403 **before** reaching the handler The handler never executes due to early 403 from `requireMFA`, which blocks the request before it reaches the route handler. The response `{ error: 'MFA verification required' }` lacks `tempToken`.

- Settings receives 403 and displays MFA prompt.
- User submits MFA code  POST `/api/auth/verify-mfa` returns 404 (endpoint missing).
  
Fixes needed:
- Remove `requireMFA` from change-password, change-email, disable-mfa routes.
- Add optional `mfaCode` parameter to these routes; verify TOTP in handlers if `mfaEnabled`.
- Alternatively, adjust `requireMFA` to accept TOTP from header/body or implement step-up token flow. The current flow requires two steps: submitting password fields first, then entering MFA code. A simpler approach would validate password and email fields in one step, and if MFA is enabled, return an `mfaRequired: true` flag along with a `stepUpToken` for the next step. This avoids the two-step process and reduces complexity.

Alternatively, the backend could return a 403 error with `mfaRequired: true` and `pendingActionToken` after password validation, allowing the frontend to handle MFA verification seamlessly. This would require changes to how the middleware and route handlers interact during password/email updates. The backend should replace `requireMFA` on sensitive routes with a new middleware or inline logic called `verifySensitiveAction`. This middleware would read `mfaCode` from the body if the user has MFA enabled, allowing the action to proceed after verification.

Alternatively, the system can return a 403 error with `mfaRequired: true` and `pendingActionToken` after password validation, enabling seamless MFA verification through the frontend.

For minimal disruption, implement:
- Replace `requireMFA` with `verifySensitiveAction` middleware that checks for `mfaCode` in the request body
- Return `stepUpToken` for MFA-enabled users to complete actions securely
- Keep existing UI behavior but simplify the flow by validating MFA during the initial request instead of requiring two separate steps

This approach maintains backward compatibility while fixing the current two-step issue. Backend should verify JWT first, then check if user has MFA enabled in Firestore. For password changes:
- If MFA is enabled and no `mfaCode` is provided, return 403 with `tempToken` (JWT with `uid` and `purpose: 'step-up-change-password'`) for frontend to use in second step.
- If `mfaCode` is present in body along with valid `tempToken`, proceed with password update after verifying TOTP.

This ensures MFA verification happens during the initial request rather than requiring two separate steps. The current flow is inconsistent. A better approach aligns with the frontend by using separate endpoints for initiating and verifying password changes.

- **POST /api/user/change-password/request** - verifies current password, issues tempToken (5 min) if MFA is enabled, returns 403 with { mfaRequired, tempToken } or proceeds if MFA is disabled.
- **POST /api/user/change-password** - removes `requireMFA` middleware. Logic:
  - Verify JWT
  - Load user and verify currentPassword
  - If user.mfaEnabled && !req.body.mfaCode, return 403 with tempToken
  - If mfaCode is present, verify TOTP and proceed with password update

This simplifies the flow into a single step where MFA verification happens during the initial request instead of requiring two separate steps. When `mfaCode` is missing during a password change, the backend should return a 403 error with `mfaRequired: true` and a `tempToken` encoding only the user ID and purpose. The new password cannot be included in the JWT due to size and security constraints, so it must be stored separatelyeither in Firestore or Redis under a pending change key, or passed via an encrypted temporary token. 

A standard pattern involves:
- Requesting `{ currentPassword, newPassword }`
- Validating current password
- Storing `newPasswordHash` in Firestore or Redis under `pendingPasswordChanges/{uid}` with expiration
- Returning a temporary token for secure confirmation

This avoids including sensitive data in tokens and ensures proper handling of MFA verification during the initial request. For Firestore without new dependencies, use a `pendingActions` collection with random token IDs and store only `newPasswordHash` in the document fields.

For a simpler approach:
- **Step 1**: On 403 response, include `tempToken` as a JWT with `uid`, `typ: 'pwd_change'`, and 10-minute expiry.
- **Step 2**: New endpoint `POST /api/user/change-password/confirm` accepts `{ tempToken, mfaCode, newPassword }`. Verify token, then verify MFA and current password before updating the password hash. Single endpoint `POST /api/user/change-password` accepts `{ currentPassword, newPassword, mfaCode? }`. If MFA is enabled and `mfaCode` is missing or invalid, return 400 with `mfaRequired`. Frontend shows MFA modal and retries the same endpoint with `mfaCode` added  one endpoint, two UI steps, no tempToken needed.

Settings.tsx will be updated to:
- On first submit: send request without `mfaCode`
- Backend: if MFA is enabled and no `mfaCode` is provided, return 403 with `{ mfaRequired: true }` (bypassing `verify-mfa` call) - On MFA verification, the same change-password endpoint is called with the mfaCode included, along with currentPassword and newPassword in state.
- Remove `requireMFA` middleware from change-password and change-email routes.
- In the change-password handler, after verifying the current password, if MFA is enabled and no valid mfaCode is provided, return a 403 error with `{ mfaRequired: true }`.
- If the mfaCode is valid or MFA is disabled, proceed with the password change.
- Fix the auth import in user.js to ensure `const { db, auth }` is properly defined.
- Implement change-email functionality with Firestore using req.user. - **reset-mfa**: Add **POST /api/user/reset-mfa** endpoint to handle MFA reconfiguration.
- For "re-configure MFA", the UI flow is: password  MFA prompt  success calls `onMfaChange` for QR setup.
- Flow should invalidate or allow setup by clearing `mfaSecret` and setting `mfaEnabled` to false, then proceed to MFA setup.
- Password verification required before resetting MFA.
- Return 403 with `mfaRequired: true` if MFA is enabled and no `mfaCode` is provided during reset. - **POST /api/user/reset-mfa**: Verifies password first; if MFA is enabled, returns 403 with `mfaRequired` for MFA code confirmation. On second submit with `mfaCode`, resets `mfaSecret`, sets `mfaEnabled` to false, and allows MFA setup.
- For two-step UI: First request with password only; if MFA is enabled, return 403 with `mfaRequired`. Second request includes `mfaCode` to finalize reset.
- Ensure `verifyJWT` middleware sets `req.user.uid` correctly.</details></think>

- validation requires `confirmPassword`

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
After resetting MFA, users must remain `accountStatus: 'active'` to continue using the app, and will need to reconfigure MFA through our designated flow. If we set `mfaRequired: true` and `accountStatus: 'pending_mfa'`, users would be locked out of login.

For reset-mfa, I'll:
- Generate new mfaSecret (base32)
- Set `mfaEnabled: false`, `mfaVerified: false`
- Keep `accountStatus: 'active'` so users can stay logged in
- Set `mfaRequired: true` only if we want to force setup

Login checks `mfaEnabled && mfaVerified` for temporary MFA - if `mfaEnabled` is false, users can log in without MFA until they complete setup again.

After reset, users should scan the QR code again. The mfa-setup in firebase-auth reads the user. The mfaSecret is regenerated from Firestore, creating a new QR code for users to scan. For reset-mfa, we set `mfaEnabled: false`, `mfaVerified: false`, and regenerate `mfaSecret`. Optionally, `mfaRequired: true` can be set on the user document.

The MfaSetup component handles this flow, calling POST /api/auth/mfa/setup with the existing mfaSecret from Firestore to generate the OTP auth URL.

During login, if both `mfaEnabled` and `mfaVerified` are false while the account remains active, the system skips MFA verification entirely, allowing users to proceed without MFA until they re-enable it. After /mfa/verify, MFA is marked as enabled and verified.

For reset-mfa:
- POST body includes password and optional mfaCode.
- Password is verified first.
- If mfaEnabled and mfaVerified are true, a valid mfaCode is required to proceed (unless user only requires password for reset).
- If mfaEnabled is true but mfaVerified is false, a TOTP code is still required if the secret exists.
- This ensures security by requiring proof of access to the old MFA device when resetting. To implement disable-mfa without requiring MFA, use a password-based approach with an optional MFA code for verification. When disabling MFA, users must provide their password and, if MFA is enabled, a valid TOTP code to confirm ownership.

For reset-mfa, export the following validation:
- `validateResetMfa`: Ensures password is provided and optional mfaCode matches format /^\d{6}$/
- Add `confirmPassword` field to match existing change-password validation

This maintains security by confirming user identity through either password alone or password plus MFA code when applicable.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
speakeasy is located in the root directory, and the backend may resolve it through node_modules hoisting. The backend has its own node_modules directory managed by pnpm, and speakeasy is not listed in backend/package.json. If speakeasy isn't present in backend/node_modules, requiring it in user.js will fail.

To fix this:
- Add speakeasy to backend/package.json dependencies
- Run `pnpm install` in the backend directory to install speakeasy in backend/node_modules
- Then require('speakeasy') in user.js should work properly Safer to add speakeasy and firebase-admin to backend/package.json if missing, though firebase-auth uses them without listing in package.json. Since backend already uses firebase in routes, it likely has firebase-admin installed in node_modules. The package.json might be incomplete.

For disable-mfa: Remove requireMFA, add inline MFA check - body needs password and if mfa enabled, mfaCode. **Validation for disable-mfa** - currently lacks express-validator; only validates password. Will add optional MFA validator inline or use sanitize + manual checks.

**Implementation of reset-mfa**:
- Requires speakeasy module
- Function `assertSensitiveMfaOk` checks if MFA is enabled
- If MFA is enabled, validates 6-digit code format
- Uses speakeasy.totp.verify with user's secret
- Returns false and sends 403 error if validation fails
- Returns true if validation passes

The implementation handles MFA verification for sensitive operations like disabling or resetting MFA. The MFA code verification uses `speakeasy.totp.verify` with a window of 2, returning false and sending a 401 error if invalid. For password change with MFA enabled, the flow should verify JWT  load user  verify current password  verify MFA if enabled. The order ensures proper validation before proceeding. 

- MFA code validation occurs with speakeasy library
- Password change requires MFA code if MFA is enabled
- Verify password and MFA in sequence during authentication
- Add optional mfaCode to change-email validation
- Normalize email address using normalizeEmail function Firestore queries for duplicate emails require checking documents with matching email addresses and ensuring the document ID differs from the user ID. For example:

- Query Firestore for documents where email equals the new email (lowercase)
- If a document exists and its ID does not match the user ID, return a 409 status code

Firebase Authentication's `updateUser` method can be used to update a user's email address in Firebase Auth.

After updating the email in Firebase Auth, the corresponding Firestore document must also be updated to reflect the new email address.

An audit log entry should be created for email changes, following the same pattern previously used for password changes.

For the disable-MFA functionality:
- Verify the JWT token
- Retrieve the user from Firestore using the UID
- Proceed with disabling MFA after successful verification and retrieval - Verify password using bcrypt against passwordHash
- If MFA is enabled, require MFA code for validation
- Update Firestore to disable MFA: set mfaEnabled to false and mfaVerified to false
- Consider clearing mfaSecret during disable; if cleared, the setup endpoint must generate a new secret
- If mfaSecret is cleared, ensure mfa/setup doesnt error due to missing secret
- Check how firebase-auth handles missing mfaSecret in mfa/setup to avoid breaking the setup flow For disable-mfa: set mfaEnabled to false, mfaVerified to false, and mfaSecret to null or leave it unchanged. This prevents login with MFA, as login checks both mfaEnabled and mfaVerified. To re-enable MFA, the signup flow uses mfaSecret from registration. On disable, generate a new speakeasy secret and store it so QR setup works, keeping mfaEnabled false until verified. The mfa/setup endpoint requires a valid mfaSecret, so generating a new one ensures setup continues to function properly. - For disable-mfa, simpler approach: keep mfaSecret but set mfaEnabled to false, allowing mfa/setup to work with existing secret.
- Better approach: remove verification flags and generate a fresh secret for next setup (similar to signup).
- Match reset-mfa behavior: generate new secret, set mfaEnabled false, mfaVerified false.
- disable-mfa handler updates:
  - Verify password using bcrypt against Firestore user
  - Apply MFA gate if needed
  - Update user document
- Use correct UID from request instead of req.user.id
- Review setup-mfa and confirm-mfa implementations in user. The frontend code uses `/api/auth/mfa/*` routes instead of old mock code, which is compatible with Firebase Authentication. For settings.tsx, remove `tempToken` from state and add `parseErrorMessage` helper for validation errors. Update `handleInitiateChange` to build request bodies per tab and include `confirmPassword` for password changes. On 403 with `data.mfaRequired`, show MFA prompt without `tempToken`. Remove separate `handleFinalVerify` endpoint and rename to `submitWithMfa`, which calls `performChangeRequest(mfaCode)`. The `performChangeRequest` function now accepts an optional `mfaCode` parameter. - Shared fetch logic uses `fetchWithAuth` with `authToken` and `uid` from context
- Email tab body includes `newEmail`, `password: emailPassword`, and `mfaCode` if present
- Password tab body includes `currentPassword`, `newPassword`, `confirmPassword`, and `mfaCode`
- MFA tab (reset) body includes `password: currentPassword` and `mfaCode`
- Success messages are displayed per tab
- On MFA tab success, `onMfaChange()` is called to navigate to MfaSetup
- `handleFinalVerify` submits with `mfaCode` from state
- 429/500 errors are handled generically
- `response.json()` is wrapped in try/catch for 404 HTML responses
- In `home.tsx`, add view 'reconfigure-mfa' for MFA setup (avoiding conflicts with existing 'mfa-setup')</details></think>

- Keep validation.js simple by adding optional mfaCode to existing validators

This approach handles empty strings properly and avoids complex conditional logic in the validator. For optional MFA code fields, use `.optional({ values: 'falsy' })` to handle empty strings properly in express-validator 7.

Validate mfaCode directly in the handler to avoid validator complexity, removing requireMFA and handling validation after sanitization.

The frontend sends all fields including mfaCode and confirmPassword, but backend should accept mfaCode alone for validation.

Note: In disable-mfa and setup-mfa, req.user.id is undefined because verifyJWT sets only uid as `{ uid, type: 'access' }`  need to adjust to use uid directly.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Identifying issues:
1. `requireMFA` checks `req.user.mfaVerified`, but JWTs only include `{ uid, type: 'access' }` — so MFA middleware always returns 403.
2. Settings calls `/api/auth/verify-mfa`, which doesn't exist (404 "Endpoint not found").
3. `change-password` never runs past middleware; `change-email` uses wrong `req.user.id` and mock password data; `auth` isn't imported in `user.js`.
4. `/api/user/reset-mfa` doesn't exist; password body omits `confirmPassword` required by validation.

Implementing MFA via TOTP in the request body (two-step UI: 403 `mfaRequired` then retry with `mfaCode`) and fixing backend routes.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
- Disable MFA functionality needs replacement with real implementation
- Use uid and Firestore for MFA handling
- Implement ensureMfaForSensitiveAction function
- Replace requireMFA with these new components When disabling MFA, users must provide password and MFA code if MFA is enabled (to prove ownership). After successful disable, Firestore fields mfaEnabled and mfaVerified are set to false. The mfaSecret can either be cleared for a fresh setup or retained for reuse during re-setup. If both mfaEnabled and mfaVerified are false, login skips MFA verification. The ensureMfaForSensitiveAction function continues to work as intended. If users want to re-enable MFA, the system will use the existing mfaSecret for convenience. For disabling MFA, a new secret will be generated to ensure security, and the fields mfaEnabled, mfaVerified, and mfaRequired will be updated accordingly. This approach ensures that login operations proceed without requiring MFA verification when both mfaEnabled and mfaVerified are false. When disabling MFA, avoid setting mfaRequired: true to prevent affecting other systems like pending_mfa account status. Keep accountStatus active and only update flags:

- mfaEnabled: false
- mfaVerified: false
- mfaRequired: false

For mfaSecret, either retain current value or generate new one during disable. If regenerating, the new secret can be used during future re-setup flows. This maintains consistency with reset behavior and prevents incomplete setup warnings.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Run pnpm install in the backend directory for speakeasy, as per user rules requiring command execution. After resetting MFA, the user's `mfaEnabled` status becomes false, meaning they won't be prompted for MFA during login until they complete `/mfa/verify` again. This behavior is expected and correct.

The login page or profile may show an incomplete state if `mfaRequired` is true, but since `accountStatus` remains `pending_mfa`, the user stays active without being blocked.

If a user changes their email via Firebase Auth, the `emailVerified` flag might need to be reset, though this isn't critical.

Running `pnpm` in the backend directory is required.

ESLint should also be run on components.

When submitting settings and receiving a 403 error due to `mfaRequired`, the system correctly does not set success  instead, the user must enter a code and retry the request. When an MFA error occurs during submission, the system properly shows the error on the MFA screen without clearing the loading state. If a 403 error occurs due to `mfaRequired`, the loading state is handled correctly and does not clear prematurely. During verification, if a valid code is entered, the MFA prompt does not reappear unnecessarily. Double submissions that succeed result in navigation away from settings, causing the component to unmount when transitioning to the MFA reconfiguration view.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Optional: document that POST /api/auth/verify-mfa was incorrect and has been resolved by discontinuing its use. - The root cause of the issue was that `requireMFA` consistently failed because JWT tokens lack an `mfaVerified` field, and the `verify-mfa` route did not exist.
- The fix involved verifying TOTP within the same user routes using `mfaCode` on retry, ensuring the backend properly handles change-email functionality, adding a reset-mfa endpoint, importing auth logic, sending `confirmPassword`, and navigating to MfaSetup upon login.
- A bonus fix corrected the flow so that logging in without MFA now goes to the correct step instead of the wrong one.
- Validation for `validateResetMfa` uses `.optional().matches` for `mfaCode`, but empty strings may still trigger validation in express-validator, potentially causing issues despite `.optional()` being intended to skip validation when undefined. If the frontend doesnt send `mfaCode`, its undefinedacceptable. If it sends an empty string (`""`), it may cause failures. The frontend only includes `mfaCode` when `includeMfa` is true and the code is exactly 6 characters longthis is correct.

For disabling MFA: `validateResetMfa` is not used, so sending an empty `mfaCode` body to a user with MFA triggers `ensureMfa`, which returns a 403 error because it sees the empty string. This behavior is consistent with API design; disabling MFA should not require a two-step process from the UI.

Git status shows that the user did not request a commit.

Another unrelated issue: signup validation uses `fullName` instead of `name`.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Root causes

1. **`requireMFA` could never pass**  
   In `backend/middleware/auth.js`, `requireMFA` checks `req.user.mfaVerified`, but access tokens from `firebase-auth` only include `{ uid, type: 'access' }`. So `mfaVerified` was always missing and every sensitive user route returned **403** before your handlers ran.

2. **Wrong MFA URL (real “Endpoint not found”)**  
   Settings called **`POST /api/auth/verify-mfa`**, which is not registered anywhere. Your API’s 404 handler returns `{ error: 'Endpoint not found' }` (see `backend/api/index.js` / `backend/server.js`). Login MFA correctly uses **`/api/auth/mfa/verify-login`**.

3. **Backend bugs**  
   - `change-password` used `auth.updateUser` without importing `auth` from `../config/firebase`.  
   - `change-email` used `req.user.id` (undefined with your JWT) and a **mock** password check, so it could never work against Firestore.  
   - **`POST /api/user/reset-mfa`** did not exist (only a comment in the UI).  
   - The app never sent **`confirmPassword`**, but validation required it, so password changes could fail validation before MFA.

## What we implemented

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| **MFA for settings** | Removed `requireMFA` from `change-email`, `change-password`, and `disable-mfa`. If the user has `mfaEnabled`, the API returns **403** with `{ mfaRequired: true }` until a valid **`mfaCode`** is sent on the **same** route (second step from your modal). TOTP is checked with `speakeasy`, same idea as login. |
| **Settings UI** | Removed the bogus `verify-mfa` call. After the MFA prompt, it **retries** the same endpoint (`change-email` / `change-password` / `reset-mfa`) including `mfaCode`, and sends **`confirmPassword`** for password changes. Clearer validation errors from `details`. |
| **`change-email`** | Uses `req.user.uid`, real password check against Firestore, duplicate email check, `auth.updateUser` + Firestore update, audit log, Firebase “email already exists” handling. |
| **`reset-mfa`** | New **`POST /api/user/reset-mfa`**: checks password + MFA when enabled, then rotates `mfaSecret` and clears MFA flags so **`/api/auth/mfa/setup`** + **`/mfa/verify`** can run again. |
| **`disable-mfa`** | Uses **`req.user.uid`**, real password + MFA gate, updates Firestore + audit log. |
| **Home** | After a successful MFA reset, opens **`MfaSetup`** instead of an alert. |
| **`MfaSetup`** | Optional **`onCancel`** → “Back to settings” when reconfiguring. |
| **`app/page.tsx`** | If login does **not** require MFA, go straight to **`home`** instead of always opening the MFA screen. |
| **`backend/package.json`** | Added **`speakeasy`** so the backend can verify TOTP even when installed on its own. |

## What you need to deploy

- **Backend**: redeploy so `user.js`, `validation.js`, and `speakeasy` are live (new route **`/api/user/reset-mfa`**).  
- **Frontend**: redeploy so `settings.tsx`, `home.tsx`, `mfa-setup.tsx`, and `app/page.tsx` are live.

Password rules match the backend: **≥12 characters** with upper, lower, number, and **`@$!%*?&`** — the settings copy mentions that so users are not surprised by validation errors.

If you want, we can next add a small **“Disable MFA”** block in settings that calls **`POST /api/user/disable-mfa`** with the same two-step pattern (it is ready on the server but not wired in the UI yet).

---

_**User**_

Okay this now being resolved there are a few things still left, 
1. The track Order feature isn't working, e.g the tracking no. we get in order summary, it doesn't work and returns network error, 
2. You can add a disable MFA button too
3. We don't have forget password option altogether, so look at these minor details of error handling and resolve them out. and after you resolve them we will finally move towards setting up blockchain hyperledger fabric

- Fall back to `http://localhost:3001` for local development

The API endpoints return different data formats: `/api/user/track-order` provides a UI-friendly format with `network`, `mobileNumber`, and `status` from the timeline's last element, while `/api/sim/track` returns `order.status` at the order level and timeline statuses in lowercase. The `track-order.tsx` component expects capitalized status values such as 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'In Transit', and 'Delivered'. For real orders, the `track-order` endpoint uses `status: orderData.timeline?.[orderData.timeline.length - 1]?.status || 'Processing'`, but **getStatusSteps** fails when comparing 'delivered' (from DB) with 'Delivered' (expected by UI), causing UI issues despite working API responses.

- Network errors require `getApiUrl` and resilient JSON parsing as primary fixes.
- Tracking numbers should use `encodeURIComponent` for path segments to handle special characters safely. Frontend uses `/api/user/track-order/` which matches the backend user.js endpoint. When fetch fails, it's due to URL/environment configuration.

- Fall back to localhost port 3001 if not set and not on localhost

- Prioritize `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` if defined, trimming and removing trailing slashes

- In production, use `window.location.origin` for same-origin relative URLs
- Return empty string if none of the above apply</details></think>

- firebase-auth.js - add routes before module.exports, need sanitize - use express.json already. For forgot password, return a generic success message ("If an account exists with these details, proceed to set a new password.") on both match and mismatch to avoid enumeration attacks. Only send the reset token if there's a match, otherwise return 200 with no token. This prevents information leakage while maintaining UX clarity. - On match: `{ success: true, resetToken }`
- On no match: `{ success: false, error: 'CNIC and email do not match any account' }`

Forforgot password, accept 13 digits or dashed format, normalize to match login's dashed format. Login uses `cnic.replace(/\D/g, '')` and maxLength 15, so user may enter digits only. Here is what was wrong and what was changed.

## 1. Track order (“network error”)

**Causes**

- In production, `getApiUrl()` returned `''`, so the browser called **`/api/...` on the Next host**. There are no Next API routes, and `next.config.mjs` had a **no-op rewrite** (`/api/*` → `/api/*`), so you often got **HTML (e.g. 404)** instead of JSON → `response.json()` threw → **“Network error”**.
- Tracking IDs like `TRK-...` must be **URL-encoded** in the path.
- Real Firestore timelines use statuses like **`delivered` / `in-transit`**, while the UI expected **`Delivered` / `In Transit`**, so the progress UI could break even when the API worked.

**Fixes**

- **`lib/utils.ts`**: If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set, it is always used (with or without localhost). If it is unset, localhost still defaults to `http://localhost:3001`; production still uses `''` **when** you rely on rewrites.
- **`next.config.mjs`**: Rewrites `/api/:path*` → `${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/api/:path*` (or `BACKEND_PROXY_URL`) when that base is set, so same-origin `/api/...` in production hits your Express API.
- **`backend/routes/user.js`**: `decodeURIComponent` on the param, **normalize** last timeline status for the UI, serialize timeline timestamps to ISO strings.
- **`components/track-order.tsx`**: `encodeURIComponent` on the tracking id, **check `Content-Type`** before `json()`, clearer errors, client-side **status normalization** and safer date handling.

**Deploy checklist**

- Set **`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`** to your API origin (no trailing slash), e.g. `https://your-api.vercel.app`, on **both** the Next app (for rewrites + optional direct calls) and wherever the client runs.
- Alternatively, set **`BACKEND_PROXY_URL`** for rewrites only and keep the client on relative `/api` (rewrites run on the server at build/runtime).

---

## 2. Disable MFA

Under **Settings → Security**, there is a segmented control: **Re-configure app** | **Disable MFA**. Disable calls existing **`POST /api/user/disable-mfa`** with the same flow as before (password, then TOTP if MFA is on). Only **re-configure** still opens the MFA setup screen after success.

`disable-mfa` now runs **`validateResetMfa`** + **`handleValidationErrors`** so bad bodies return proper validation errors instead of failing silently.

---

## 3. Forgot password + login error handling

**Backend (`firebase-auth.js`)**

- **`POST /api/auth/forgot-password`**: Body `{ cnic, email }`. CNIC is normalized to `XXXXX-XXXXXXX-X`, user must exist and **email must match** the account. Responds with a **15-minute JWT** (`purpose: 'password_reset'`) — no email provider required.
- **`POST /api/auth/reset-password`**: Body `{ resetToken, newPassword, confirmPassword }`. Updates **Firebase Auth** + Firestore `passwordHash`, **deletes Firestore sessions** so old refresh tokens stop working.

**Frontend**

- New **`components/forgot-password.tsx`**: two steps (verify identity → new password).
- **`app/page.tsx`**: step `forgot-password`.
- **`login.tsx`**: “Forgot password?”, **formats CNIC** to dashed 13-digit form before login (matches `validateLogin`), safer **JSON** handling, **`loginNotice`** green banner after a successful reset (via `sessionStorage`).

**`backend/middleware/validation.js`**: `validateForgotPasswordRequest`, `validatePasswordResetConfirm`.

---

When you move to **Hyperledger Fabric**, we can wire registration/audit paths to channels and chaincode using your existing `submitToBlockchain` hooks and env/config you already have in the repo.

---

_**User**_

what changes i have to make to make track order work

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
To make the track order feature work, you need to ensure the following changes and configurations are in place:

- Verify that the `track_order` field is properly defined in your data model or database schema.
- Confirm that the API endpoints or backend logic correctly handle the `track_order` parameter when creating or updating tracks.
- Ensure the frontend UI allows users to input or modify track order values.
- Check that any relevant middleware or validation rules support the new `track_order` functionality.
- Test the end-to-end flow to confirm that track order is saved and retrieved correctly.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Track order fails when the browser cannot reach your **Express API** as JSON at `/api/user/track-order/...`. After the code changes, you mainly need **environment + deploy** alignment.

### 1. Set the API base URL (required in most setups)

On **Vercel** (or wherever the **Next.js** app runs), set:

- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`** = your backend origin, **no trailing slash**  
  Example: `https://your-backend.up.railway.app`

That does two things:

- The **client** can call `https://…/api/...` directly when this is set (see `getApiUrl()` in `lib/utils.ts`).
- **Rewrites** in `next.config.mjs` proxy same-origin `/api/*` to `${NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/api/*` when the app is opened in production without using the full URL in the client.

Redeploy the **frontend** after changing env vars (rewrites are evaluated at build/runtime depending on your host).

### 2. Backend must expose that route

Your API must actually serve:

`GET /api/user/track-order/:trackingNumber`

(i.e. Express mounted with `/api/user` and the `track-order` route — already in your repo.)

Redeploy the **backend** if you only updated the Next app.

### 3. If the API is on another domain and you do **not** use rewrites

Then the browser calls the **full** `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`. Your API must allow **CORS** for your Next app’s origin (your `backend/api/index.js` / `server.js` already uses permissive CORS in the Vercel-style app — confirm the server you deploy matches that).

### 4. Optional: proxy-only env

If you prefer rewrites to use a different URL than the public client env, set:

- **`BACKEND_PROXY_URL`** = internal API URL for rewrites only  

(`next.config.mjs` prefers `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`, then `BACKEND_PROXY_URL`.)

---

**Quick check:** Open the site, DevTools → Network, search for `track-order`.  
- **200** + JSON → OK.  
- **404 HTML** from your Next domain → fix **`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`** + redeploy and/or rewrites.  
- **CORS error** → enable CORS on the API or use same-origin **rewrites** only (`getApiUrl()` `''` in prod + rewrite to backend).

No extra code changes are required beyond what’s already merged if the above env and deploys are correct.

---

            "text": " * Register new SIM (requires MFA and biometric verification)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": " */"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "router.post("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "  '/register',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "  verifyJWT,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "  requireMFA,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  sanitizeInput,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "  validateSIMRegistration,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "  handleValidationErrors,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "  async (req, res) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    try {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "      const {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        mobileNetwork,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "        mobileNumber,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "        deliveryAddress,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "        paymentAddress,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "        sameAsDelivery,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "        fingerprintImages: rawFingerprintImages,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        fingerprintImage: legacyFingerprintImage,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "      } = req.body;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "      const fingerprintImages = Array.isArray(rawFingerprintImages)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "        ? rawFingerprintImages"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "        : legacyFingerprintImage ? [legacyFingerprintImage] : [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "      const userId = req.user.id;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "      const cnic = req.user.cnic;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "      // Verify fingerprint biometric against NADRA records"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "      const biometricResult = await verifyFingerprintWithNadra(cnic, fingerprintImages);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "      if (!biometricResult.verified) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "        return res.status(403).json({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "          error: 'Biometric verification failed',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "          message: 'Fingerprint does not match NADRA biometric records. Please ensure you are the legitimate CNIC holder.',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "          matchScore: biometricResult.matchScore,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "        });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "      }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "      // Check if user has 5 active SIMs (enforced by smart contract)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "      // const activeSIMsResult = await fabricClient.evaluateTransaction("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "      //   'getActiveSIMCount',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "      //   cnic"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "      // );"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "      // const activeSIMsData = JSON.parse(activeSIMsResult);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "      // if (activeSIMsData.activeSIMCount \u003e= 5) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "      //   return res.status(403).json({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "      //     error: 'Maximum 5 SIMs allowed per CNIC',"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "      //     message: 'Please deactivate an existing SIM to register a new one'"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "      //   });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "      // }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "      // Generate transaction and tracking IDs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "      const transactionId = uuidv4();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "      const trackingNumber = crypto.randomBytes(12).toString('hex').toUpperCase();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "      // Prepare SIM registration data"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "      const simRegistration = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "        transactionId"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": ","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "      };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "  }"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# DIMS-SR Backend Implementation Guide"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "## Overview"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "This document outlines the complete backend architecture for the Digital Identity Management System - SIM Registration with Hyperledger Fabric blockchain integration."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "## Architecture Components"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "### 1. Hyperledger Fabric Network"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "**Location**: &#96;/docker-compose-fabric.yml&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "**Components**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "- **Orderer Node**: Manages transaction ordering and block creation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- **Peer Node (Org1)**: Processes transactions and maintains world state"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "- **CouchDB**: State database for efficient data retrieval"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- **CLI**: Command-line interface for network administration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "**Key Features**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Multi-organization network setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- Private data collections support"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- Event streaming capabilities"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "### 2. Smart Contracts (Chaincode)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "**Location**: &#96;/chaincode/sim-registry/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "**Functions Implemented**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "1. &#96;registerSIM()&#96; - Register new SIM with validation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "   - Age verification (18+ years)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "   - CNIC format validation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "   - 5 SIM per CNIC limit enforcement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "   - Generates transaction ID and tracking number"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "2. &#96;deactivateSIM()&#96; - Deactivate registered SIM"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "   - Ownership verification"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "   - Status update to inactive"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "   - Event emission"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "3. &#96;getSIMsByCNIC()&#96; - Retrieve all SIMs for a user"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "   - Query by CNIC identifier"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "   - Returns active and inactive SIMs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "4. &#96;getActiveSIMCount()&#96; - Check registration limits"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "   - Returns count of active SIMs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "   - Determines if new SIM can be registered"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "5. &#96;getTransactionHistory()&#96; - Audit trail"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "   - Complete transaction history"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "   - Timestamp and state change tracking"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "6. &#96;verifyCNIC()&#96; - CNIC validation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "   - Format verification"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "   - Integration point with government database"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "### 3. Node.js Express API"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "**Location**: &#96;/backend/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "**Structure**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "backend/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "├── server.js              # Main server with security middleware"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "├── package.json           # Dependencies"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "├── .env.example           # Environment configuration template"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "├── middleware/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "│   ├── auth.js           # JWT and session management"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "│   └── validation.js     # Input validation and sanitization"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 68
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./BACKEND_IMPLEMENTATION.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# DIMS-SR (Digital Identity Management System - SIM Registration) - Complete Project"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "**Status**: ✅ FULLY IMPLEMENTED - Ready for Integration and Deployment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "## Project Overview"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "DIMS-SR is a comprehensive Digital Identity Management System for SIM Registration built with enterprise-grade security, blockchain integration, and cross-platform support. The system is built on Hyperledger Fabric blockchain with a Node.js/Express backend, PostgreSQL database, and native mobile/desktop applications."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "## Deliverables Summary"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "### 1. Frontend Web Application (Already Completed)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "**Location**: &#96;/app&#96;, &#96;/components&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "**Features**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Responsive UI for mobile, tablet, and desktop (320px+)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Complete authentication flow (signup, login, MFA setup)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- SIM registration with multi-step process"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "- View registered SIMs with deactivation capability"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "- Track order functionality"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "- User settings (email, password, MFA management)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- 5 SIM limit enforcement per CNIC"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- Professional design with consistent theming"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "**Tech Stack**: Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "### 2. Backend API with Security"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,

          {
            "lineNumber": 336
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 337,
            "text": "## Setup Instructions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 339,
            "text": "### Step 1: Install Dependencies"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 341,
            "text": "cd backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 342,
            "text": "npm install"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 343,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 344
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 345,
            "text": "### Step 2: Generate SSL/TLS Certificates"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 346,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 347,
            "text": "chmod +x scripts/generate-certs.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 348,
            "text": "./scripts/generate-certs.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 349,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 350
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 351,
            "text": "### Step 3: Configure Environment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 352,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 353,
            "text": "cp .env.example .env"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 354,
            "text": "# Edit .env with your configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 355,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 356
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 357,
            "text": "### Step 4: Setup Database"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 358,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 359,
            "text": "# Start PostgreSQL first"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 360,
            "text": "chmod +x scripts/setup-db.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 361,
            "text": "./scripts/setup-db.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 362,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 363
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 364,
            "text": "### Step 5: Setup Hyperledger Fabric"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 365,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 366,
            "text": "cd .."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 367,
            "text": "docker-compose -f docker-compose-fabric.yml up -d"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 368,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 369
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 370,
            "text": "### Step 6: Deploy Chaincode"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 371,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 372,
            "text": "# Deploy sim-registry chaincode to fabric network"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 373,
            "text": "# Instructions in chaincode directory"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 374,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 375
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 376,
            "text": "### Step 7: Start API Server"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 377,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 378,
            "text": "cd backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 379,
            "text": "npm start  # Production"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 380,
            "text": "npm run dev  # Development"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 383,
            "text": "## Blockchain Integration Points"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 384
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 385,
            "text": "1. **User Registration**: Create user record in DB and blockchain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 386,
            "text": "2. **SIM Registration**: Store SIM on immutable ledger with 5-SIM limit enforcement"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 387,
            "text": "3. **Age Verification**: Chaincode validates user is 18+"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "4. **CNIC Validation**: Format check and integration with government DB"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 389,
            "text": "5. **SIM Deactivation**: Update status on blockchain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 390,
            "text": "6. **Audit Trail**: All actions logged to blockchain"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 391
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 392,
            "text": "## Next Steps"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 393
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "1. **Complete Database Integration**: Connect to PostgreSQL"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 395,
            "text": "2. **Hyperledger Fabric Connection**: Setup fabric-network SDK"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 396,
            "text": "3. **Mobile Application**: React Native with same API endpoints"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 397,
            "text": "4. **Desktop Application**: Electron wrapper for web app"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 398,
            "text": "5. **Production Deployment**: Use proper CA certificates"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 399,
            "text": "6. **Security Audits**: Penetration testing and code review"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 400
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 401,
            "text": "## Files Created"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 402
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 403,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 404,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 405,

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 497
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 498,
            "text": "## 🚀 Quick Start"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 499
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 500,
            "text": "### Prerequisites"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 501,
            "text": "- Node.js 16+"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 502,
            "text": "- PostgreSQL 12+"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 503,
            "text": "- Docker \u0026 Docker Compose"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 504
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 505,
            "text": "### Installation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 506,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 507,
            "text": "# Install dependencies"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 508,
            "text": "npm install"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 509
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 510,
            "text": "# Setup database"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 511,
            "text": "cd backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 512,
            "text": "chmod +x scripts/setup-db.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 513,
            "text": "./scripts/setup-db.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 514
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 515,
            "text": "# Generate SSL certificates"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 516,
            "text": "chmod +x scripts/generate-certs.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 517,
            "text": "./scripts/generate-certs.sh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 518
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 519,
            "text": "# Start Hyperledger Fabric"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 520,
            "text": "cd .."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 521,
            "text": "docker-compose -f docker-compose-fabric.yml up -d"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 522
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 523,
            "text": "# Start backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 524,
            "text": "cd backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 525,
            "text": "npm start"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 526
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 527,
            "text": "# Start frontend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "npm run dev"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 531,
            "text": "### Environment Configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 532,
            "text": "Create &#96;.env&#96; file in &#96;/backend&#96;:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 533,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;env"
          },
          {

            "text": "PORT=3001"
          },

            "text": "NODE_ENV=production"
          },

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 538,
            "text": "DB_USER=dims_user"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 539,
            "text": "DB_PASSWORD=\u003cpassword\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 540,
            "text": "FABRIC_CHANNEL_NAME=dims-sr-channel"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 541,
            "text": "SSL_CERT_PATH=./certs/server.crt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 542,
            "text": "SSL_KEY_PATH=./certs/server.key"
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 546
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 547,
            "text": "## 📋 API Endpoints Summary"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 548
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 549,
            "text": "| Endpoint | Method | Description | Auth Required |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 550,
            "text": "|----------|--------|-------------|---------------|"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 551,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/auth/signup&#96; | POST | User registration | ❌ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 552,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/auth/login&#96; | POST | User authentication | ❌ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 553,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/auth/verify-mfa&#96; | POST | MFA verification | ❌ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 554,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/auth/refresh-token&#96; | POST | Token refresh | ✅ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 555,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/sim/register&#96; | POST | SIM registration | ✅ + MFA |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 556,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/sim/deactivate&#96; | POST | Deactivate SIM | ✅ + MFA |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 557,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/sim/registered&#96; | GET | Get user's SIMs | ✅ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 558,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/sim/track/:id&#96; | GET | Track order | ❌ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 559,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/user/profile&#96; | GET | User profile | ✅ |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 560,
            "text": "| &#96;/api/user/change-password&#96; | POST | Password change | ✅ + MFA |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 561
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 562,
            "text": "---"
          }
        ],
